title.source: Setting up the registration process and registration questions
title.target: Setting up the registration process and registration questions

body:0001.source: This article is machine translated from english
body:0001.target: This article is machine translated from english

body:0002.source: Why is the registration process important?
body:0002.target: Why is the registration process important?

body:0003.source: A well-designed registration process is key to creating a smooth experience for new users. Keeping the form short, simple, and clear helps boost user satisfaction and reduces drop-offs.
body:0003.target: A well-designed registration process is key to creating a smooth experience for new users. Keeping the form short, simple, and clear helps boost user satisfaction and reduces drop-offs.

body:0004.source: Before you start building your registration form, ask yourself:
body:0004.target: Before you start building your registration form, ask yourself:

body:0005.source: What demographic data do I need to collect from all users?
body:0005.target: What demographic data do I need to collect from all users?

body:0006.source: What information is only relevant for certain participants or projects?
body:0006.target: What information is only relevant for certain participants or projects?

body:0007.source: How to trigger the registration flow?
body:0007.target: How to trigger the registration flow?

body:0008.source:
From the Homepage
Trigger the registration flow by clicking the Log In or Register button on the homepage.
body:0008.target:
From the Homepage
Trigger the registration flow by clicking the Log In or Register button on the homepage.

body:0009.source:
Using a Direct URL
Access the registration screen directly by visiting https://<platform_domain>/sign-up, which will display the registration popup.
body:0009.target:
Using a Direct URL
Access the registration screen directly by visiting https://<platform_domain>/sign-up, which will display the registration popup.

body:0010.source:
Before Participating in a Phase
If Email Confirmation or Account Creation is set as a prerequisite for participation, the registration flow will trigger when the user clicks the participation button (e.g., Submit Survey, Submit Idea, Vote, etc.).
body:0010.target:
Before Participating in a Phase
If Email Confirmation or Account Creation is set as a prerequisite for participation, the registration flow will trigger when the user clicks the participation button (e.g., Submit Survey, Submit Idea, Vote, etc.).

body:0011.source:
After Participating in a Phase
If None is selected as a prerequisite to participate, users can participate without registering. After submission, they will be prompted to register to stay informed.
body:0011.target:
After Participating in a Phase
If None is selected as a prerequisite to participate, users can participate without registering. After submission, they will be prompted to register to stay informed.

body:0012.source: How to set up the registration process?
body:0012.target: How to set up the registration process?

body:0013.source: Create a smooth onboarding experience by customising your platform’s registration steps.
body:0013.target: Create a smooth onboarding experience by customising your platform’s registration steps.

body:0014.source: The registration flow consists of three steps:
body:0014.target: The registration flow consists of three steps:

body:0015.source: User initiates the sign-up form by entering their email address
body:0015.target: User initiates the sign-up form by entering their email address

body:0016.source: User verifies their email address, then logs in successfully
body:0016.target: User verifies their email address, then logs in successfully

body:0019.source: You can configure demographic questions for users to fill in
body:0019.target: You can configure demographic questions for users to fill in

body:0023.source: You can a description to inform users via Settings > Registration > Enter Email step
body:0023.target: You can a description to inform users via Settings > Registration > Enter Email step

body:0024.source: Below is an example image of the view users will get to see.
body:0024.target: Below is an example image of the view users will get to see.

body:0025.source: If the user has entered an existing email address they will in a following step be asked to enter their password. If there is no existing email address the registration flow will continue at step 2.
body:0025.target: If the user has entered an existing email address they will in a following step be asked to enter their password. If there is no existing email address the registration flow will continue at step 2.

body:0026.source: ℹ️ Note: You can directly link users to the sign-up form by using the following URL: https://<your platform domain>/sign-up
body:0026.target: ℹ️ Note: You can directly link users to the sign-up form by using the following URL: https://<your platform domain>/sign-up

body:0028.source: By default users will be prompted to verify their email address when registering on the platform.
body:0028.target: By default users will be prompted to verify their email address when registering on the platform.

body:0030.source: If you want to disable email verification on your platform, contact support via the chat bubble at the bottom right of your screen
body:0030.target: If you want to disable email verification on your platform, contact support via the chat bubble at the bottom right of your screen

body:0032.source: You can a description to inform users via Settings > Registration > Complete your profile step
body:0032.target: You can a description to inform users via Settings > Registration > Complete your profile step

body:0033.source: Below is an example image of the view users will get to see.
body:0033.target: Below is an example image of the view users will get to see.

body:0035.source: After email verification, users can be shown demographic questions. These can be set as optional or required, depending on your needs.
body:0035.target: After email verification, users can be shown demographic questions. These can be set as optional or required, depending on your needs.

body:0037.source: If enabled, users can select topics and areas they want to receive updates about, you can add these via:
body:0037.target: If enabled, users can select topics and areas they want to receive updates about, you can add these via:

body:0040.source: When users select preferences they will receive email notifications when a project is published/updated that has been tagged with one of their preferences.
body:0040.target: When users select preferences they will receive email notifications when a project is published/updated that has been tagged with one of their preferences.

body:0041.source: ℹ️ Users can update their follow preferences or unsubscribe anytime via Account Settings > Notifications or Activity > Following.
body:0041.target: ℹ️ Users can update their follow preferences or unsubscribe anytime via Account Settings > Notifications or Activity > Following.

body:0042.source: How to customize your registration questions?
body:0042.target: How to customize your registration questions?

body:0043.source: Add, remove, or adjust the questions asked during user registration.
body:0043.target: Add, remove, or adjust the questions asked during user registration.

body:0046.source: Year of birth: used for age group insights on dashboards
body:0046.target: Year of birth: used for age group insights on dashboards

body:0047.source: Place of residence: linked to geographic areas (Areas tab in the Platform Settings), useful for access control and representation analysis
body:0047.target: Place of residence: linked to geographic areas (Areas tab in the Platform Settings), useful for access control and representation analysis

body:0051.source: You can add a description to clarify what you are asking.
body:0051.target: You can add a description to clarify what you are asking.

body:0053.source: You can add these types of questions:
body:0053.target: You can add these types of questions:

body:0061.source: How many people live in your household? (numeric)
body:0061.target: How many people live in your household? (numeric)

body:0062.source: ⚠️ For the representativeness dashboard only single select questions can be used.
body:0062.target: ⚠️ For the representativeness dashboard only single select questions can be used.

body:0064.source: Select the question where you want to add options to
body:0064.target: Select the question where you want to add options to

body:0069.source: Access Control: Control participation based on user traits
body:0069.target: Access Control: Control participation based on user traits

body:0070.source: Group Segmentation: Sort users into smart or standard groups
body:0070.target: Group Segmentation: Sort users into smart or standard groups

body:0071.source: Data Consistency: Only registration fields appear across all dashboards and reports
body:0071.target: Data Consistency: Only registration fields appear across all dashboards and reports

body:0074.source: Only default registration fields can be used in AI analysis
body:0074.target: Only default registration fields can be used in AI analysis

body:0075.source: Only single-choice questions show up in demographic graphs
body:0075.target: Only single-choice questions show up in demographic graphs

body:0076.source: "Year of birth" cannot currently be used for grouping survey answers by age
body:0076.target: "Year of birth" cannot currently be used for grouping survey answers by age

body:0079.source: Participation projects: View demographic participation per project.
body:0079.target: Participation projects: View demographic participation per project.

body:0080.source: AI analysis and Auto-insights: Segment insights based on default demographic fields.
body:0080.target: AI analysis and Auto-insights: Segment insights based on default demographic fields.

body:0082.source: What are project level demographic questions?
body:0082.target: What are project level demographic questions?

body:0084.source: By default, new projects inherit platform-level registration questions
body:0084.target: By default, new projects inherit platform-level registration questions

body:0086.source: Choose which questions apply to their project
body:0086.target: Choose which questions apply to their project

body:0088.source: To modify this, go to the phase settings and check the Phase access and user data section.
body:0088.target: To modify this, go to the phase settings and check the Phase access and user data section.

body:0089.source: ⚠️ Project managers can select registration questions that are not enabled on the platform level, however, the questions need to be created by a platform admin in the registration section of the platform settings. After this, a project manager can ask this as a specific question for their project.
body:0089.target: ⚠️ Project managers can select registration questions that are not enabled on the platform level, however, the questions need to be created by a platform admin in the registration section of the platform settings. After this, a project manager can ask this as a specific question for their project.

body:0090.source: To learn more about access rights specifically, read our article: Understanding Access Rights
body:0090.target: To learn more about access rights specifically, read our article: Understanding Access Rights
